stories.title: A Super-Fast Cable Links Singapore and Indonesia Under the Sea
stories.headline_kid: Singapore and Indonesia just finished building a special underwater cable that will make the internet between the two countries faster than ever!
summary: Imagine a super long wire, thinner than your finger, lying quietly on the ocean floor, carrying millions of messages every second. That's what an undersea cable does! Singapore and Indonesia have just completed one that connects Changi in Singapore to Nongsa Digital Park in Batam, Indonesia.

This new cable is the shortest and most direct link between the two places. It will be ready to use by September 2026. Nongsa Digital Park is becoming a busy centre for data centres, which are like giant storage rooms for all the photos, videos and information we use online.

Singapore's Deputy Prime Minister Gan Kim Yong and Indonesia's minister Airlangga Hartarto called it a 'new digital corridor'. That means it's like a bridge — but instead of cars, it carries internet data quickly between the two countries.

This matters because faster, more reliable internet helps businesses, schools and families in both countries. It also shows how Singapore and its neighbours are working together on modern technology, not just old things like trade in goods.

lenses.me: If you use apps or watch videos that store data in this region, this cable could make things load faster and smoother.
lenses.family: If your family's business or work connects with Indonesia, this cable could make things run more efficiently and maybe even cheaper.
lenses.friends: If you have friends or relatives in Batam, video calls and online games with them might work better now.
lenses.nation: It boosts Singapore's role as a digital hub in Southeast Asia, which could bring more tech jobs here.
lenses.world: It's an example of how countries build shared technology instead of just competing — a lesson in cooperation.

compo_words.meaning: found under the ocean
compo_words.example: The undersea cable stretched for many kilometres beneath the waves.

compo_words.meaning: a passage that connects two places
compo_words.example: The new digital corridor links Singapore and Batam closely.

compo_words.meaning: basic systems like roads, cables or pipes that a country needs
compo_words.example: Good infrastructure helps a country grow quickly.

compo_words.meaning: to work together with others
compo_words.example: Singapore and Indonesia collaborated to build the cable.

compo_phrases.meaning: a technology link that connects two places like a bridge connects two lands
compo_phrases.example: The cable acts as a digital bridge between the two nations.
The new cable will boost ties between Singapore and Indonesia.

questions: How do you think faster internet would change the way you do your homework or watch videos?
Why do you think it's useful for Singapore to work closely with its neighbours like Indonesia?
Can you think of another example where two countries work together to build something useful?
phrases: I think this is important because...
One good thing about this is...
If I were in charge, I would...

sources.name: CNA - 'New digital corridor': Singapore, Indonesia say Batam-Changi undersea cable's arrival will boost ties

stories.title: Southeast Asian Leaders Meet to Solve Big Regional Problems
stories.headline_kid: Foreign ministers from 10 Southeast Asian countries, including Singapore, met in the Philippines to discuss serious issues like food, energy and safety in our region.
summary: ASEAN is a group of 10 Southeast Asian countries — including Singapore, Malaysia, Indonesia and the Philippines — that work together like teammates on a big project. This week, their foreign ministers gathered in Manila, the capital of the Philippines, for an important meeting.
They talked about several serious challenges. One is 'energy and food security', which simply means making sure every country has enough fuel and food for its people, even during tough times.

They also discussed the ongoing crisis in Myanmar, a neighbouring country facing internal conflict, and rising tensions in the South China Sea, where several countries argue over who owns certain waters and islands.

Singapore sends its own foreign minister to these meetings to represent our country's views and interests. Even though Singapore is small, being part of ASEAN gives us a stronger voice when discussing regional problems.

lenses.me: It shows how countries, like classmates in a group project, need to talk and cooperate to solve shared problems.
lenses.family: Decisions made at meetings like this can affect prices of food and fuel that your family buys.
lenses.friends: Understanding regional issues helps you have better conversations with friends about the news.
lenses.nation: Singapore's participation helps protect our trade routes and safety in the region.
lenses.world: It's a good example of how neighbouring countries try to solve problems through talking instead of fighting.

compo_words.meaning: the state of being safe and protected
compo_words.example: Food security means everyone has enough to eat.

compo_words.meaning: managing relationships between countries through discussion
compo_words.example: Diplomacy helps countries solve problems without conflict.

compo_words.meaning: a feeling of unease or disagreement between groups
compo_words.example: There is tension between countries over the disputed waters.

compo_words.meaning: to work together
compo_words.example: ASEAN countries cooperate to keep the region peaceful.

compo_phrases.example: The ministers agreed to work hand in hand on food security.
ASEAN hopes talks will help keep the peace in the region.

questions: Why do you think it's important for countries to talk to each other instead of arguing?
How do you think problems in one country, like Myanmar, can affect its neighbours?
Can you think of a time when working together with classmates solved a problem better than working alone?

sources.name: CNA - Regional issues high on the agenda as ASEAN foreign ministers meet in Manila

stories.title: America Adds a Big Extra Tax on Goods from Canada
stories.headline_kid: The US President just added a huge 50% extra charge on many goods from Canada, and trade disputes like this can make things pricier around the world — even in Singapore.
summary: A tariff is like an extra tax added onto something bought from another country. If a toy from Canada normally costs $10, a 50% tariff could make it cost $15 once it reaches America.

US President Donald Trump signed an order to add this new 50% tariff on many Canadian goods, like wine, hockey sticks and cement. He says Canada has treated American products, like dairy and alcohol, unfairly.

This is part of bigger trade tensions between countries. The US also recently made rules to make it harder for its defence companies to get materials like rare earths — special minerals used in phones, cars and even missiles — from countries like China.

Even though Singapore isn't directly involved, we trade with many countries around the world. When two big countries argue over trade, it can affect the price of goods and materials that eventually reach us too, since global trade is like a giant interconnected web.

lenses.me: If prices of imported toys, snacks or gadgets rise because of trade disputes, your allowance might not stretch as far.
lenses.family: Your family's shopping bills for imported goods could slowly increase if trade tensions continue.
lenses.friends: Things you and your friends enjoy, like certain snacks or electronics, might be affected by these bigger economic changes.
lenses.nation: Singapore, as a trading hub, depends on smooth global trade — disruptions like this can affect businesses here.
lenses.world: It shows how trade disagreements between two countries can create ripple effects that reach many other nations.

compo_words.meaning: an extra tax on goods bought from another country
compo_words.example: The new tariff made imported wine more expensive.

compo_words.meaning: to officially put a rule or tax in place
compo_words.example: The government imposed a tariff on foreign goods.

compo_words.meaning: a disagreement or argument
compo_words.example: The trade dispute between the two countries lasted for months.

compo_words.meaning: to change often, going up and down
compo_words.example: Prices of imported goods may fluctuate due to tariffs.

compo_phrases.meaning: an additional charge added onto a price
compo_phrases.example: Consumers may end up paying an extra tax on imported items.

compo_phrases.meaning: when one event causes a chain of other effects
compo_phrases.example: The tariff caused a ripple effect across many industries.

questions: How do you think rising prices might affect the things your family buys?
Why do you think countries sometimes argue about trade rules?
If you were a leader, how would you solve a trade disagreement peacefully?
phrases: I think it's important to consider...

sources.name: CNA - Trump orders new 50% tariff on many Canadian goods
CNA - US seeks to limit key materials from China, others in defence supply chains

stories.title: Heavy Rains Cause Deadly Floods and Landslides in India
stories.headline_kid: Monsoon rains caused terrible floods and landslides in India, sadly killing at least 19 people.
summary: In parts of South Asia, there's a season called the monsoon, when very heavy rain falls for weeks. It's a bit like Singapore's rainy season, but much, much stronger and longer-lasting.
This weekend, the heavy rains caused floods in Nagaland, a state in northeast India near Myanmar, killing eight people. In another area called Jammu and Kashmir, landslides killed 11 more people.

A landslide happens when heavy rain soaks into a hillside, making the soil and rocks loose and slippery. Imagine a wet sandcastle slowly sliding apart — that's similar to what happens on a much bigger, more dangerous scale.

Rescue teams are working hard to search for survivors and help affected families. Sadly, monsoon disasters like this happen almost every year in parts of India, reminding us how powerful nature can be.

lenses.me: It helps you understand that weather can sometimes be very dangerous, not just something to complain about when it rains on your outdoor activities.
lenses.family: If your family has relatives or friends in India, this news might make you want to check in on their safety.
lenses.friends: You could talk to friends about how different countries experience weather differently and why some places are riskier.
lenses.nation: Singapore is generally safer from landslides because of our flatter land, but we can learn about disaster preparedness from such events.
lenses.world: It shows how climate patterns can bring serious harm, and why many countries are working on better disaster warning systems.

compo_words.meaning: a season of very heavy rainfall in parts of Asia
compo_words.example: The monsoon brought days of non-stop rain to the region.

compo_words.meaning: when soil and rocks slide down a slope, usually after heavy rain
compo_words.example: A landslide blocked the mountain road after the storm.

compo_words.meaning: to move people away from a dangerous place to safety
compo_words.example: Villagers had to evacuate before the floodwaters rose higher.

compo_words.meaning: a sudden, terrible event causing great damage
compo_words.example: The floods were a catastrophe for many families.

compo_phrases.meaning: to cause great damage or destruction
compo_phrases.example: The heavy rains wreaked havoc on the villages nearby.

compo_phrases.meaning: the effort to find and save people in danger
compo_phrases.example: Search and rescue teams worked through the night.

questions: How would you feel if your home was affected by floods like the ones in India?
Why do you think some places are more likely to experience landslides than others?
What can people do to prepare for extreme weather like heavy monsoon rains?
phrases: This must be very difficult because...
I think preparation could help by...

sources.name: CNA - India floods and landslides kill at least 19

stories.title: Spain Crowned World Cup Champions — Fans Everywhere, Including Singapore, Celebrate!
stories.headline_kid: Spain became the world's football champions after beating Argentina in a thrilling World Cup final, and football fans in Singapore stayed up late to watch it too!
summary: The World Cup is the biggest football tournament in the world, held once every four years, where teams from many countries compete to be champions. This year, Spain beat Argentina in the final, played in New Jersey, USA, on 19 July.
After the win, huge crowds gathered in Madrid, Spain's capital, cheering and chanting 'Kings of the World' as players celebrated with a victory parade the next day.

However, not everything was joyful. Right after the match, players from both teams got into a scuffle on the pitch. FIFA, the organisation that runs world football, is now investigating what happened to decide if any punishment is needed.

Even though Singapore didn't have a team in the tournament, many Singaporean families and friends stayed up late — since matches were often shown here at odd hours due to time differences — just to enjoy the excitement together. It shows how football brings people from every corner of the world together.

lenses.me: You might have stayed up late with your family to watch the final, feeling the excitement even from far away.
lenses.family: Big matches like this are a fun reason for families to gather together, even late at night.
lenses.friends: You and your friends probably had lots to talk about — favourite players, the final score, or even the post-match scuffle.
lenses.nation: Singapore doesn't have a World Cup team yet, but our passion for football shows we're part of this global sporting community.
lenses.world: The World Cup unites millions of fans everywhere, showing how sport can bring joy across different cultures and countries.

compo_words.meaning: feeling or showing great joy from winning
compo_words.example: The triumphant Spanish team paraded through Madrid's streets.

compo_words.meaning: an impressive public event or display
compo_words.example: The World Cup final was a thrilling spectacle for fans worldwide.

compo_words.meaning: a strong competition between two teams or people
compo_words.example: The rivalry between Spain and Argentina made the final exciting.

compo_words.meaning: fair and honourable behaviour in sports, especially towards opponents
compo_words.example: Good sportsmanship means respecting your opponents even after losing.

compo_phrases.meaning: united people through a shared event
compo_phrases.example: The World Cup final brought fans together from every continent.

compo_phrases.meaning: behaving humbly and kindly even when you win
compo_phrases.example: True champions show grace in victory, not just skill.

questions: Do you think how players behave after a match matters as much as winning? Why?
Have you ever stayed up late to watch or take part in something exciting with your family? What was it like?
Why do you think sports like football can bring people from different countries together?
phrases: This reminds me of a time when...
In my opinion, winning isn't everything because...

sources.name: CNA - Scores celebrate Spain's 'Kings of the World' win in exhilarated Madrid
CNA - FIFA to investigate World Cup final post-match brawl
